fact: December 28 is exactly 363 days into the year, which means only 2 days are left before the new year starts!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when the sun sets super early - sometimes before 5 PM - making it feel like nighttime comes in the afternoon.
Polar bears in the Arctic are walking on ice that's getting thicker every day around this time, and some ice can hold up to 1,000 pounds per square foot!
December 28 falls during Capricorn season, and people born on this day are supposed to be extra good at climbing things - just like mountain goats!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, December 28 is peak summer, so kids might be having beach barbecues while kids up north are building snowmen.
The moon around this time can look extra bright and crisp because cold winter air has less moisture to blur the view - it's like nature's HD TV!
Evergreen trees like pine and spruce are actually growing their strongest roots right now, even though everything looks frozen above ground.
In cold places, your breath creates those cool fog clouds because the air temperature can be 60+ degrees colder than your body temperature.
Owls are extra active around this date because small animals leave more tracks in fresh snow, making them easier to hunt.
The Earth is actually closest to the sun around this time of year - only about 91 million miles away instead of the usual 93 million!
December 28 happens during the week when many flowers that will bloom in spring are already forming tiny buds underground, even in frozen soil.
